uncovered_requirements.text: /nf:debug Step A.5 runs formal model consultation before quorum dispatch, appending $FORMAL_CONTEXT to the worker bundle
Quorum worker prompts include a rule to respect fix constraints and not violate listed invariants
NEXT STEP result table includes a FORMAL row showing model verdict (reproduced/not-reproduced/no-model)
When no model covers the failure, debug Step G appends an entry to bug-model-gaps.json for future model creation
/nf:debug absorbs model-driven-fix Phases 1-4 (discovery, reproduction, refinement, constraint extraction)
Loop 1 (autoresearch-refine.cjs) runs natively within debug flow for bug reproduction
Extracted constraints from Loop 1 feed back into debug fix guidance and quorum worker prompts
Debug skill produces a reproducing formal model as a deliverable (not just a fix)
nf:mcp-repair calls all MCP identity, health_check, and deep_health_check tools directly in the orchestrator, never via Task() sub-agents which lack access to the parent session's MCP servers.
nf:mcp-repair discovers slot names dynamically from ~/.claude.json mcpServers at runtime instead of using hardcoded enumeration, allowing the tool to work with any slot configuration.
nf:mcp-status banner text SHALL compute agent counts dynamically from ~/.claude.json mcpServers instead of hardcoding provider counts
MCP server dynamically detects installed CLI providers at startup and excludes slots whose binaries are not found, using deduplication and fail-open logic to handle errors gracefully.
close-formal-gaps.md accepts `--bug-context` flag that injects failure description into spec generation, biasing toward capturing the failure mode
Model refinement loop creates/refines a model, verifies it FAILS (reproduces the bug), and iterates up to 2 additional times if model passes despite bug
model-driven-fix.md workflow orchestrates the 6-phase prescriptive cycle (discovery, reproduction, refinement, constraint extraction, constrained fix, pre-verification)
selectSlot delegates to pluggable 3-tier policy interface enabling learned slot routing via PresetPolicy (Tier 0) and RiverPolicy (Tier 1) contextual bandit with confidence gates.
RiverPolicy implements Q-learning with Bellman updates, epsilon-greedy exploration, and learning rate decay to optimize slot selection based on historical task outcomes.

E2E learning loop test validates full River ML pipeline (rewards recorded → Q-table updated → learned preference → shadow recommendation) and nf-statusline displays shadow recommendations with confidence
